//: Pinned away from SDK 10.0.301, whose Razor compiler cannot parse a component inside a code
block: `@if (x) { <MudText>…</MudText> }` is read as C# rather than markup, producing a
cascade of nonsense errors ('Severity is a type but is used like a variable') pointing at
columns that do not correspond to the source. Reproduced on a stock `dotnet new blazor`
with the built-in <PageTitle>, so it is not specific to this project or to MudBlazor.
rollForward latestPatch keeps 10.0.2xx patches available while refusing 10.0.3xx. Retest and
raise this once a fixed 10.0.3xx ships.
The pin also exists on principle: without a global.json the toolchain drifts under you. This
surfaced when a background SDK update landed mid-session and builds that had been passing
began failing on untouched, already-deployed code.
